Assistant.getDefault.name: Default Assistant
Assistant.getDefault.description: General-purpose AI assistant for everyday tasks
Assistant.getDefault.instructions: You are a helpful, harmless, and honest AI assistant. Provide clear, accurate, and concise responses to user queries. If you are unsure about something, say so rather than making up information.

AssistantTemplate.getDefault.name: Default Assistant
AssistantTemplate.getDefault.description: General-purpose AI assistant for everyday tasks
AssistantTemplate.getDefault.instructions: You are a helpful, harmless, and honest AI assistant. Provide clear, accurate, and concise responses to user queries. If you are unsure about something, say so rather than making up information.

assistants.name: Default Assistant
assistants.description: General-purpose AI assistant for everyday tasks
assistants.instructions: You are a helpful, harmless, and honest AI assistant. Provide clear, accurate, and concise responses to user queries. If you are unsure about something, say so rather than making up information.

available.description: Shell + coreutils + curl + jq + git + python3 (interpreter only).
minimal + numpy + pandas + torch + R 4.4 + tidyverse + Node 24 (npx) + uv (uvx) + ts-node.

Hub.getAssistants.name: io.github.phibya/code-reviewer
Hub.getAssistants.description: Expert code reviewer with a security-and-best-practices focus.
Hub.getAssistants.instructions:
You are an expert code reviewer. Focus on security, performance, maintainability,
and best practices. Provide constructive feedback with specific, actionable suggestions.
When you flag an issue, cite the file path and line number, explain the failure mode,
and propose a concrete fix.
Hub.getAssistants.author: Ziee Team

Hub.getAssistants.name: io.github.phibya/creative-writer
Hub.getAssistants.description: Professional creative writer for stories, articles, and content.
Hub.getAssistants.instructions:
You are a professional creative writer with expertise in storytelling, content
creation, and engaging writing. Create compelling, well-structured content tailored
to the user's tone, audience, and length requirements.
Hub.getAssistants.author: Ziee Team

Hub.getAssistants.name: io.github.phibya/deep-researcher
Hub.getAssistants.description: Multi-step research assistant. Pinned to a future ziee-chat that ships the parallel sub-agent runtime — used to exercise the compat gate.
Hub.getAssistants.instructions:
You are a meticulous research assistant. Decompose the question into
sub-questions, gather evidence for each, cite sources, and synthesize a
balanced answer. Flag uncertainty explicitly and distinguish established
facts from speculation.
Hub.getAssistants.author: Ziee Team

Hub.getAssistants.name: io.github.phibya/sql-helper
Hub.getAssistants.description: Writes, explains, and optimizes SQL across Postgres, MySQL, and SQLite dialects.
Hub.getAssistants.instructions:
You are a SQL expert. Help users write correct, efficient, and readable SQL.
When asked for a query: state your assumptions about the schema, give the
query, then a one-line explanation of what it does and any indexes it relies
on. Prefer standard SQL; call out dialect-specific syntax when you use it.
Never run destructive statements without flagging them first.
Hub.getAssistants.author: Ziee Team

Hub.getAssistants.name: io.github.phibya/vision-analyst
Hub.getAssistants.description: Specialized assistant for image and visual content analysis.
Hub.getAssistants.instructions:
You are an expert in visual content analysis. Help users understand images,
extract information, and provide detailed visual descriptions. When analyzing,
describe composition, subject, mood, and notable details — then answer the
user's specific question.
Hub.getAssistants.author: Ziee Team

items.name: app.linear/mcp
items.title: Linear MCP Server
items.summary: Manage Linear issues, projects, and cycles over Streamable HTTP.

items.name: com.brave/search-mcp
items.title: Brave Search MCP Server
items.summary: Web search via the Brave Search API for up-to-date information.

items.name: io.github.github/mcp
items.title: GitHub MCP Server
items.summary: Access GitHub repositories, issues, and pull requests over the official GitHub MCP endpoint.

items.name: io.github.modelcontextprotocol/filesystem
items.title: Filesystem MCP Server
items.summary: Local filesystem access (read/write/search) over MCP (stdio).

items.name: io.github.modelcontextprotocol/memory
items.title: Memory & Context MCP Server
items.summary: Store and retrieve conversation context, notes, and knowledge across sessions.

items.name: io.github.modelcontextprotocol/postgres
items.title: PostgreSQL MCP Server
items.summary: Connect to PostgreSQL databases, execute queries, manage schemas, and analyze data.

items.name: io.github.phibya/deepseek-r1-70b
items.title: DeepSeek-R1 70B
items.summary: Large reasoning model. Flagged as requiring a future ziee for its tensor-parallel runtime support.

items.name: io.github.phibya/llama-3-1-8b-instruct
items.title: Llama 3.1 8B
items.summary: Meta's Llama 3.1 8B instruction-tuned model with improved capabilities.

items.name: io.github.phibya/llama-3-2-3b-instruct-gguf
items.title: Llama 3.2 3B
items.summary: Meta's Llama 3.2 3B instruction-tuned model in GGUF format. Multiple quantizations available.

items.name: io.github.phibya/nomic-embed-text-v1-5-gguf
items.title: Nomic Embed Text v1.5
items.summary: High-quality general-purpose English embedding model (768-dim) packaged as GGUF for llama.cpp.

items.name: io.github.phibya/phi-3-mini-4k-instruct
items.title: Phi-3 Mini
items.summary: Microsoft's small but capable instruction-tuned model. Great for low-resource setups.

items.name: io.github.phibya/qwen2.5-coder-7b-instruct
items.title: Qwen2.5-Coder 7B
items.summary: Alibaba's Qwen2.5-Coder 7B — code generation, completion, and reasoning across 40+ languages.

items.name: io.github.phibya/qwen2.5-vl-3b-instruct
items.title: Qwen2.5-VL 3B
items.summary: Qwen2.5-VL 3B instruction-tuned vision-language model. Supports image understanding plus tool use.

items.name: io.github.ziee/answer-with-citations
items.title: Answer With Citations
items.summary: Confirm scope, fan-out search queries, synthesize a cited answer.

items.name: io.github.ziee/code-review
items.title: Code Review
items.summary: Walks git diff, reviews each file via LLM, synthesizes findings.

items.name: io.github.ziee/data-transform-pipeline
items.title: Data Transform Pipeline
items.summary: LLM detects shape, writes a Python transform script, sandbox runs + validates.

items.name: io.github.ziee/deep-research
items.title: Deep Research
items.summary: Multi-stage research pipeline -- query gen, fan-out search, cross-verify, outline, memo.

items.name: io.github.ziee/document-from-outline
items.title: Document From Outline
items.summary: Parse outline, fan-out write each section in parallel, assemble final doc.

items.name: io.github.ziee/extract-and-classify
items.title: Extract and Classify
items.summary: Chunk text, extract per chunk in parallel, classify aggregated extractions.

items.name: io.github.ziee/git-commit-summarize
items.title: Git Commit Summarize
items.summary: Capture git diff + status, draft a conventional-commits message.

items.name: io.github.ziee/research-summarize-write
items.title: Research, Summarize, Write
items.summary: Three-step llm chain: research a topic, summarize sources, write a memo.

items.name: io.github.ziee/web-page-to-markdown
items.title: Web Page to Markdown
items.summary: Fetch a URL, extract readable content, polish to clean markdown.

items.name: io.github.ziee/effective-prompting
items.title: Effective Prompting
items.summary: Model-agnostic techniques for clearer, more reliable LLM prompts — structure, output shaping, few-shot, and hallucination control.

items.name: io.github.ziee/sr-review
items.title: SR: Systematic Review (durable)
items.summary: End-to-end systematic review in one durable run: search + LLM auto-expansion + AI screen, a human screening gate, open-access full-text extraction, a human review gate, and a cited evidence synthesis.

connectors.keyless_note: Works without a key — biomedical literature + preprints.
Works without a key; add a contact email to join the polite pool (higher rate limit).

key_field.label: Crossref Plus token
key_field.help: Optional — Crossref Plus subscribers get higher rate limits.

config_fields.label: Contact email (polite pool)
config_fields.help: Joins Crossref's polite pool (~10 req/s vs ~5 anonymous) AND enables Unpaywall, the DOI→open-access-PDF resolver used by fetch_paper_fulltext — without it, DOI-only papers can't resolve full text.

connectors.keyless_note: Works without a key (shared pool — may rate-limit); add a free key for a dedicated rate.

key_field.label: Semantic Scholar API key
key_field.help: Free; sent as x-api-key. Recommended for reliable throughput.

connectors.keyless_note: Works without a key (3 req/s); add a free NCBI key for 10 req/s.

key_field.label: NCBI API key
key_field.help: Free — raises the rate limit from 3 to 10 req/s.

config_fields.label: Contact email
config_fields.help: NCBI asks API users to identify themselves.

connectors.keyless_note: Works without a key — CS / methods / quant-bio preprints.
Requires a free CORE API key.

key_field.label: CORE API key
key_field.help: Free — register at core.ac.uk; sent as a bearer token.

key_field.label: Crossref Plus token
key_field.help: Optional — Crossref Plus subscribers get higher rate limits.

key_field.label: Semantic Scholar API key
key_field.help: Free; sent as x-api-key. Recommended for reliable throughput.

key_field.label: NCBI API key
key_field.help: Free — raises the rate limit from 3 to 10 req/s.

key_field.label: CORE API key
key_field.help: Free — register at core.ac.uk; sent as a bearer token.

servers.name: bio
servers.description: Built-in biomedical database connectors (PubMed, ClinicalTrials.gov, variants, trials, drugs, and more) via BioMCP. Connected-only: query terms are sent to public upstream APIs. Configure deployment API keys (NCBI_API_KEY, S2_API_KEY, OPENFDA_API_KEY, NCI_API_KEY, ONCOKB_TOKEN, ALPHAGENOME_API_KEY, DISGENET_API_KEY) as secret entries in this server's Headers; they are injected into the managed sidecar's environment, never sent over HTTP.

servers.name: workflow
servers.description: Built-in workflow execution (one tool per installed workflow)

projects.name: UI Showcase
projects.description: Demo project that hosts the showcase conversation.
projects.instructions: You are a rendering test fixture. Prefer rich, varied formatting.

skills.name: io.github.ziee/configure-code-sandbox
skills.description: Enable + configure ziee's bwrap-isolated code execution sandbox. Use when the user wants the LLM to run code, install Python packages, or asks about sandbox flavors.
skills.when_to_use: User asks about code execution, sandbox, bwrap, "can the LLM run my script", flavor downloads, Python in chat.

frontmatter_json.description: Enable + configure ziee's bwrap-isolated code execution sandbox. Use when the user wants the LLM to run code, install Python packages, or asks about sandbox flavors.
frontmatter_json.name: configure-code-sandbox
frontmatter_json.when_to_use: User asks about code execution, sandbox, bwrap, "can the LLM run my script", flavor downloads, Python in chat.

skills.name: io.github.ziee/configure-llm-providers
skills.description: Configure LLM providers (Anthropic, OpenAI, Groq, Ollama, local) in ziee. Use when the user mentions adding an API key, switching models, getting "no providers configured" errors, or asks how to use a specific model.
skills.when_to_use: User asks about LLM provider setup, API key configuration, "how do I add my key", connecting Ollama, picking a model, or seeing "no model selected" warnings.

frontmatter_json.description: Configure LLM providers (Anthropic, OpenAI, Groq, Ollama, local) in ziee. Use when the user mentions adding an API key, switching models, getting "no providers configured" errors, or asks how to use a specific model.
frontmatter_json.name: configure-llm-providers
frontmatter_json.when_to_use: User asks about LLM provider setup, API key configuration, "how do I add my key", connecting Ollama, picking a model, or seeing "no model selected" warnings.

skills.name: io.github.ziee/configure-mcp-servers
skills.description: Install + configure MCP servers in ziee. Use when the user wants to add a tool to the LLM (filesystem, web search, github, etc.) or asks about MCP.
skills.when_to_use: User mentions MCP, adding tools, "how do I let the LLM read files", filesystem access, web search integration, github tools.

frontmatter_json.description: Install + configure MCP servers in ziee. Use when the user wants to add a tool to the LLM (filesystem, web search, github, etc.) or asks about MCP.
frontmatter_json.name: configure-mcp-servers
frontmatter_json.when_to_use: User mentions MCP, adding tools, "how do I let the LLM read files", filesystem access, web search integration, github tools.

skills.name: io.github.ziee/create-skill
skills.description: Author a new skill for ziee (Agent Skills standard -- works in Claude Code too). Use when the user wants to create, edit, or share a skill.
skills.when_to_use: User asks "how do I make a skill", wants to create knowledge for the LLM, asks about SKILL.md, mentions Agent Skills spec.

frontmatter_json.description: Author a new skill for ziee (Agent Skills standard -- works in Claude Code too). Use when the user wants to create, edit, or share a skill.
frontmatter_json.name: create-skill
frontmatter_json.when_to_use: User asks "how do I make a skill", wants to create knowledge for the LLM, asks about SKILL.md, mentions Agent Skills spec.

skills.name: io.github.ziee/create-workflow
skills.description: Author a new workflow for ziee (declarative YAML DAG with llm / llm_map / sandbox / elicit step kinds). Use when the user wants to create, edit, or test a workflow.
skills.when_to_use: User asks "how do I make a workflow", wants to chain LLM calls, mentions workflow.yaml, asks about steps, kind, llm_map, sandbox steps.

frontmatter_json.description: Author a new workflow for ziee (declarative YAML DAG with llm / llm_map / sandbox / elicit step kinds). Use when the user wants to create, edit, or test a workflow.
frontmatter_json.name: create-workflow
frontmatter_json.when_to_use: User asks "how do I make a workflow", wants to chain LLM calls, mentions workflow.yaml, asks about steps, kind, llm_map, sandbox steps.

skills.name: io.github.ziee/hub-installation
skills.description: Browse + install models, assistants, MCP servers, skills, workflows from the ziee hub. Use when the user asks about the hub, installing something from a catalog, or finding tools to extend ziee.
skills.when_to_use: User mentions hub, registry, catalog, browsing tools, installing skills/workflows/MCP servers, "where do I find more X".

frontmatter_json.description: Browse + install models, assistants, MCP servers, skills, workflows from the ziee hub. Use when the user asks about the hub, installing something from a catalog, or finding tools to extend ziee.
frontmatter_json.name: hub-installation
frontmatter_json.when_to_use: User mentions hub, registry, catalog, browsing tools, installing skills/workflows/MCP servers, "where do I find more X".

skills.name: io.github.ziee/install-samtools-bcftools
skills.description: Install samtools and bcftools (SAM/BAM/CRAM/VCF/BCF processing) into the code sandbox via bioconda. Use when the user works with sequencing alignment or variant files and these tools aren't installed.
skills.when_to_use: User mentions BAM/SAM/CRAM/VCF/BCF files, read alignments, variant calling, "samtools", "bcftools", "index my BAM", pileup, or genomics file conversion.

frontmatter_json.description: Install samtools and bcftools (SAM/BAM/CRAM/VCF/BCF processing) into the code sandbox via bioconda. Use when the user works with sequencing alignment or variant files and these tools aren't installed.
frontmatter_json.name: install-samtools-bcftools
frontmatter_json.when_to_use: User mentions BAM/SAM/CRAM/VCF/BCF files, read alignments, variant calling, "samtools", "bcftools", "index my BAM", pileup, or genomics file conversion.

skills.name: io.github.ziee/manage-projects
skills.description: Create/manage chat projects in ziee -- group conversations + attach reference files + set per-project assistant + MCP defaults. Use when the user wants to organize conversations or attach files to multiple chats at once.
skills.when_to_use: User mentions projects, wants to attach files, organize conversations, set defaults for related chats.

frontmatter_json.description: Create/manage chat projects in ziee -- group conversations + attach reference files + set per-project assistant + MCP defaults. Use when the user wants to organize conversations or attach files to multiple chats at once.
frontmatter_json.name: manage-projects
frontmatter_json.when_to_use: User mentions projects, wants to attach files, organize conversations, set defaults for related chats.

skills.name: io.github.ziee/rnaseq-toolkit
skills.description: Install a bulk RNA-seq toolkit (STAR, salmon, HISAT2, featureCounts/subread, samtools) into the code sandbox via bioconda. Use when the user runs RNA-seq alignment or quantification and the tools aren't installed.
skills.when_to_use: User mentions RNA-seq, transcript quantification, read alignment for expression, STAR, salmon, HISAT2, featureCounts, subread, or "FASTQ to counts".

frontmatter_json.description: Install a bulk RNA-seq toolkit (STAR, salmon, HISAT2, featureCounts/subread, samtools) into the code sandbox via bioconda. Use when the user runs RNA-seq alignment or quantification and the tools aren't installed.
frontmatter_json.name: rnaseq-toolkit
frontmatter_json.when_to_use: User mentions RNA-seq, transcript quantification, read alignment for expression, STAR, salmon, HISAT2, featureCounts, subread, or "FASTQ to counts".

skills.name: io.github.ziee/setup-datascience-env
skills.description: Bootstrap a persistent Python data-science environment (numpy/pandas/scipy/matplotlib/scikit-learn) inside the code sandbox using micromamba. Use when the user wants Python data analysis, plotting, or scientific computing in chat and the needed packages aren't already installed.
skills.when_to_use: User asks to analyze a CSV/dataframe, plot data, run pandas/numpy/scipy/sklearn, or "set up a Python environment". Run this before reaching for those libraries if an import fails.

frontmatter_json.description: Bootstrap a persistent Python data-science environment (numpy/pandas/scipy/matplotlib/scikit-learn) inside the code sandbox using micromamba. Use when the user wants Python data analysis, plotting, or scientific computing in chat and the needed packages aren't already installed.
frontmatter_json.name: setup-datascience-env
frontmatter_json.when_to_use: User asks to analyze a CSV/dataframe, plot data, run pandas/numpy/scipy/sklearn, or "set up a Python environment". Run this before reaching for those libraries if an import fails.

skills.name: io.github.ziee/set-up-memory
skills.description: Enable + use ziee's per-user persistent memory (mem0-style fact storage with vector retrieval). Use when the user wants the LLM to remember things across conversations.
skills.when_to_use: User mentions "remember this", asks why the LLM forgot something, wants persistent context, or asks about the memory feature.

frontmatter_json.description: Enable + use ziee's per-user persistent memory (mem0-style fact storage with vector retrieval). Use when the user wants the LLM to remember things across conversations.
frontmatter_json.name: set-up-memory
frontmatter_json.when_to_use: User mentions "remember this", asks why the LLM forgot something, wants persistent context, or asks about the memory feature.

skills.name: io.github.ziee/troubleshoot-workflow-run
skills.description: Diagnose a failed workflow run using log resources + the error structure. Use when a workflow tool call returned is_error or a run failed.
skills.when_to_use: User reports "my workflow failed", asks why a tool errored, mentions a workflow run that didn't produce expected output, or the LLM just got an is_error tool_result from a workflow tool call.

frontmatter_json.description: Diagnose a failed workflow run using log resources + the error structure. Use when a workflow tool call returned is_error or a run failed.
frontmatter_json.name: troubleshoot-workflow-run
frontmatter_json.when_to_use: User reports "my workflow failed", asks why a tool errored, mentions a workflow run that didn't produce expected output, or the LLM just got an is_error tool_result from a workflow tool call.

skills.name: io.github.ziee/use-assistants
skills.description: Use + create assistants in ziee -- preset combinations of system prompt + model + parameters. Use when the user wants a specialized chat persona or asks about assistants.
skills.when_to_use: User mentions assistant, wants a persona, asks "what's the difference between chat and assistant", wants to save a prompt as a reusable assistant.

frontmatter_json.description: Use + create assistants in ziee -- preset combinations of system prompt + model + parameters. Use when the user wants a specialized chat persona or asks about assistants.
frontmatter_json.name: use-assistants
frontmatter_json.when_to_use: User mentions assistant, wants a persona, asks "what's the difference between chat and assistant", wants to save a prompt as a reusable assistant.

groups.name: Administrators
groups.description: System administrators with full access to all features
